title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, and Ambiguity · Language Lens: Ambiguous Publish Recovery
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must enable confirms before publishing, drain confirmation channels, bound outstanding sequence numbers, and reconcile close ambiguity; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ambiguous publish recovery increment in a bounded Go confirm-window ledger. Learners pr…
objectives: Classify unresolved confirms after failure as ambiguous, retain stable message identity, and republish only through a duplicate-safe downstream contract; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Explain when confirms occur for unroutable messages, persistent classic queues, quorum queues, and multiple routed queues; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Ambiguous Publish Recovery · Theory Ambiguous Publish Recovery
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 2524 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound outstanding confirms and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; reject stale connection and channel generations; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
